title: Data Catalogs Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of data catalog metadata, lineage, DataHub, and governance practices.

questions.question: What specific type of metadata does 'data lineage' in a catalog capture?

options.text: The original source code of the ETL pipeline that created the table
The upstream tables/pipelines that produce a dataset and the downstream reports/dashboards that consume it
The column-level descriptions and business glossary terms for a table
The version history of schema changes over time

questions.question: Why is it important to integrate data catalog registration into CI/CD pipelines rather than doing it manually?

options.text: CI/CD pipelines are faster than manual registration tools
Manual metadata registration is always forgotten — automation ensures the catalog stays current automatically every time a pipeline runs or a schema changes
Regulatory compliance requires automated catalog updates in all industries
Manual registration doesn't support column-level metadata

questions.question: What does a 'data tier' classification (Bronze/Silver/Gold) in a catalog indicate?

options.text: The cost tier of the cloud storage used to store the data
The data quality and transformation level: Bronze = raw/ingested, Silver = cleaned, Gold = business-ready aggregations
The access level required to query the data (Bronze = public, Gold = restricted)
The age of the data — Bronze is recent, Gold is archival

questions.question: Why is column-level lineage more valuable than table-level lineage for PII compliance?

options.text: Column-level lineage is easier to compute than table-level lineage
It allows auditors to trace exactly which columns containing sensitive data flow into which downstream reports, dashboards, or ML models
Table-level lineage doesn't work across different database platforms
Column-level lineage automatically encrypts PII fields in the catalog

questions.question: What does the DataHub ingestion recipe YAML's `profiling.enabled: true` setting typically collect?

options.text: CPU and memory usage profiles of the query engine
Statistical summaries of tables such as row counts, column null rates, min/max values, and top values — used for data quality assessments
Query execution plans for the top 10 most expensive queries
User access patterns and which engineers query which tables most frequently
